real_insight: The senior engineer's two disciplines — architectural reading (what is invariant, what is implementation) and shipping discipline (the iteration loop and its hygiene) — are usually treated as one budget on engineering teams. The verse authorizes naming them separately and protecting them separately. The first quarter where both are explicitly planned and tracked looks measurably different from prior quarters because work no longer collapses into a single substrate where neither discipline is fully practiced.
objection: Could be misread as 'every engineering job has two halves and you should do both' — that would be platitude. The engineering layer is more specific: it identifies the structural problem (teams collapse the two into one budget), the operational forums where each lives (architecture review channel for the first; GitHub merge cadence and Sentry error budgets for the second), and the consequence (action no longer reduces the underlying problem when the disciplines collapse). The META structure of the verse — Krishna naming the structure of his own teaching — maps to the engineering staff/principal naming the structure of the team's work, which is itself the load-bearing engineering move.
